title: Strict Typing, Nullability, and Boundary Validation · Spiral Review
summary: Establish a strict type baseline without confusing static types with runtime validation. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a strict type and boundary contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enable the language or checker strict baseline and document intentional deviations.
Model null, missing, unknown, and unchecked indexed access explicitly.
Validate files, network data, environment variables, and user input at runtime.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Strict Mode · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates typing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates typing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Strict Mode without dropping earlier contracts?

content.text: Enable the language or checker strict baseline and document intentional deviations. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates typing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Strict Mode, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a strict type and boundary contract.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Gradual typing has value only when every value is annotated. This fails the Strict Mode contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: Ruff Rules (Current 2026-07-13) — Controls Python linting, unused code, safe fixes, suppression review, and rule selection.

stimulus.title: Typing Migration case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates typing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A giant first-pass strict migration is the only honest approach.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Ratchet strictness without permanently exempting legacy areas. Artifact under review: a strict type and boundary contract.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Typing Migration, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Typing Migration applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Ratchet strictness without permanently exempting legacy areas.
